{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Electronics Technician",
  "description": "Schlumberger Limited is the world&#39;s leading provider of technology for reservoir characterization, drilling, production, and processing to the oil and gas industry. Working in more than 85 countries and employing people who represent over 140 nationalities, Schlumberger supplies the industry&#39;s most comprehensive range of products and services, from exploration through production and integrated pore-to-pipeline solutions for hydrocarbon recovery that optimize reservoir performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Electronics Technician

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full time

Summary


	We are looking for an experienced Senior Electronic Technician with a strong background in industrial electronics, control systems, and oilfield equipment.
	The candidate will be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting advanced electronic and acquisition systems.


Responsibilities


	Maintain and troubleshoot electrical, electronic, and data acquisition systems
	Service and repair electronic systems of nitrogen pumps
	Perform maintenance on coiled tubing equipment and high-pressure fracturing well service pumps
	Calibrate and maintain densitometers and flowmeters
	Diagnose and repair internal combustion engine systems
	Manage and support local area networks (LAN)
	Utilize CMMS systems for maintenance planning and documentation.


Exposures and Experiences


	Diploma or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Electrical &amp; Electronics Engineering
	Minimum of 7 years of relevant experience
	Strong expertise in industrial electronics and instrumentation
	Experience with LAN systems and data acquisition technologies
	Knowledge of oilfield equipment maintenance
	Technical training certification (required)
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-28",

Senior Electronics Technician Job at Schlumberger Limited




        
    

        
        Schlumberger Limited is the world's leading provider of technology for reservoir characterization, drilling, production, and processing to the oil and gas industry. Working in more than 85 countries and employing people who represent over 140 nationalities, Schlumberger supplies the industry's most comprehensive range of products and services, from exploration through production and integrated pore-to-pipeline solutions for hydrocarbon recovery that optimize reservoir performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Electronics Technician

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full time

Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for an experienced Senior Electronic Technician with a strong background in industrial electronics, control systems, and oilfield equipment.
	The candidate will be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting advanced electronic and acquisition systems.


Responsibilities


	Maintain and troubleshoot electrical, electronic, and data acquisition systems
	Service and repair electronic systems of nitrogen pumps
	Perform maintenance on coiled tubing equipment and high-pressure fracturing well service pumps
	Calibrate and maintain densitometers and flowmeters
	Diagnose and repair internal combustion engine systems
	Manage and support local area networks (LAN)
	Utilize CMMS systems for maintenance planning and documentation.


Exposures and Experiences


	Diploma or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Electrical & Electronics Engineering
	Minimum of 7 years of relevant experience
	Strong expertise in industrial electronics and instrumentation
	Experience with LAN systems and data acquisition technologies
	Knowledge of oilfield equipment maintenance
	Technical training certification (required)
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control Supervisor",
  "description": "Schlumberger Limited is the world&#39;s leading provider of technology for reservoir characterization, drilling, production, and processing to the oil and gas industry. Working in more than 85 countries and employing people who represent over 140 nationalities, Schlumberger supplies the industry&#39;s most comprehensive range of products and services, from exploration through production and integrated pore-to-pipeline solutions for hydrocarbon recovery that optimize reservoir performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Supervisor

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full time

Description


	The Quality Control Supervisor is responsible for ensuring equipment testing and post-maintenance quality control are performed prior to green tagging or releasing equipment to the Asset Coordinator.
	The Quality Control Supervisor manages daily operation and maintenance of the test facility and manages test facility personnel.
	The Quality Control Supervisor is also responsible for ensuring the Quality Management System and required processes (plant procedures, quality plans, etc) are established, implemented and maintained in accordance with API, ISO, ASTM, etc.


Responsibilities


	Manage the test and quality control (QC) organization.
	Optimize and standardize test and QC processes, procedures and capabilities.
	Manage the tracking and calibration of inspection, measurement and test equipment as well as maintenance of the test facility equipment.
	Periodic technical audits and CAT compliance assessments, while ensuring maintenance related remedial action plan closures.
	Perform technical audits of vendors and suppliers to ensure compliance with and qualification to the Quality Management System
	Develop a continuous improvement culture, initiatives and improvement plans in the assigned location or GeoUnit to improve asset utilization, reliability and cost of service delivery (CoSD).
	Participate in failure investigations as assigned by the GeoUnit Reliability and CoSD or Maintenance Manager and with other functions, ensure that root causes of defects are addressed systematically and expediently.
	Provide quality data books and Certifications of Conformity (COC) to products and services delivered to the customers.


Experience &amp; Exposures


	HND / B.Sc in Engineering &ndash; Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, Petroleum, Materials/Metallurgy.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience (inclusive of 2 years in shop operations) in oilfield Completions or other oilfield disciplines, including leadership/supervisory roles held.
	Hands-on competency with Completions technologies for Sand Management Services, Intelligent Completions, and Liner Hangers is an added advantage
	Experience with Maintenance Processes, Workflows and Quality Control
	Knowledge of tubulars, material types, connection thread types, termination fittings, and test fixtures.
	Knowledge of CMMS and Maintenance Methodologies used in the oilfield.
	Knowledge of oilfield QHSE standards and guidelines.
	Strong sense of continuous improvement with relevant certifications and executed projects.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-27",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-28",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Quality Control Supervisor Job at Schlumberger Limited




        
    

        
        Schlumberger Limited is the world's leading provider of technology for reservoir characterization, drilling, production, and processing to the oil and gas industry. Working in more than 85 countries and employing people who represent over 140 nationalities, Schlumberger supplies the industry's most comprehensive range of products and services, from exploration through production and integrated pore-to-pipeline solutions for hydrocarbon recovery that optimize reservoir performance.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Supervisor

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Job Type: Full time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Quality Control Supervisor is responsible for ensuring equipment testing and post-maintenance quality control are performed prior to green tagging or releasing equipment to the Asset Coordinator.
	The Quality Control Supervisor manages daily operation and maintenance of the test facility and manages test facility personnel.
	The Quality Control Supervisor is also responsible for ensuring the Quality Management System and required processes (plant procedures, quality plans, etc) are established, implemented and maintained in accordance with API, ISO, ASTM, etc.


Responsibilities


	Manage the test and quality control (QC) organization.
	Optimize and standardize test and QC processes, procedures and capabilities.
	Manage the tracking and calibration of inspection, measurement and test equipment as well as maintenance of the test facility equipment.
	Periodic technical audits and CAT compliance assessments, while ensuring maintenance related remedial action plan closures.
	Perform technical audits of vendors and suppliers to ensure compliance with and qualification to the Quality Management System
	Develop a continuous improvement culture, initiatives and improvement plans in the assigned location or GeoUnit to improve asset utilization, reliability and cost of service delivery (CoSD).
	Participate in failure investigations as assigned by the GeoUnit Reliability and CoSD or Maintenance Manager and with other functions, ensure that root causes of defects are addressed systematically and expediently.
	Provide quality data books and Certifications of Conformity (COC) to products and services delivered to the customers.


Experience & Exposures


	HND / B.Sc in Engineering – Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, Petroleum, Materials/Metallurgy.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience (inclusive of 2 years in shop operations) in oilfield Completions or other oilfield disciplines, including leadership/supervisory roles held.
	Hands-on competency with Completions technologies for Sand Management Services, Intelligent Completions, and Liner Hangers is an added advantage
	Experience with Maintenance Processes, Workflows and Quality Control
	Knowledge of tubulars, material types, connection thread types, termination fittings, and test fixtures.
	Knowledge of CMMS and Maintenance Methodologies used in the oilfield.
	Knowledge of oilfield QHSE standards and guidelines.
	Strong sense of continuous improvement with relevant certifications and executed projects.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

About the Role


	We are seeking a smart, proactive, and result-driven Operations/Marketing Officer who will play a key role in daily operations and directly source new catering contracts to grow the business.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee and coordinate daily operations for catering and camp services
	Drive business development efforts by sourcing and securing new catering jobs
	Build and maintain strong client relationships across all service areas
	Prepare quotations, proposals, and detailed business reports
	Develop and execute effective marketing and growth strategies
	Monitor operational expenses and support cost-control initiatives
	Ensure strict compliance with HSE and food safety standards
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports for management.

	3 &ndash; 6 years of proven experience in catering operations or similar roles
	Strong communication, negotiation, and organizational skills
	Knowledge of cost control, marketing strategies, and reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Experience in industrial catering services is a strong advantage
	Age: 35&ndash;40 years.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@careerbreedgeconsults.com and careerbreedgeconsultancy@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Operations / Marketing Officer Job at Career Breedge Consultancy Limited




        
    

        
        Career Breedge Consultancy - Our client is an expanding industrial catering, housekeeping, and laundry service company operating both offshore and onshore.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations / Marketing Officer

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking a smart, proactive, and result-driven Operations/Marketing Officer who will play a key role in daily operations and directly source new catering contracts to grow the business.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee and coordinate daily operations for catering and camp services
	Drive business development efforts by sourcing and securing new catering jobs
	Build and maintain strong client relationships across all service areas
	Prepare quotations, proposals, and detailed business reports
	Develop and execute effective marketing and growth strategies
	Monitor operational expenses and support cost-control initiatives
	Ensure strict compliance with HSE and food safety standards
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports for management.


Requirements


	HND / B.Sc Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Hospitality Management, or a related field
	3 – 6 years of proven experience in catering operations or similar roles
	Strong communication, negotiation, and organizational skills
	Knowledge of cost control, marketing strategies, and reporting
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Experience in industrial catering services is a strong advantage
	Age: 35–40 years.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Software Engineer",
  "description": "Global Aluminium Industries is a manufacturer of aluminum building components. The company is engaged in manufacturing and marketing building products including aluminium doors, windows, railings and suspended ceilings.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Software Engineer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are looking for a passionate Software Engineer to design, develop and install software solutions.
	Software Engineer responsibilities include gathering user requirements, defining system functionality and writing code in various languages, like Java, Ruby on Rails or .NET programming languages (e.g. C++ or JScript.NET.) Our ideal candidates are familiar with the software development life cycle (SDLC) from preliminary system analysis to tests and deployment.


Responsibilities


	Execute full software development life cycle (SDLC)
	Develop flowcharts, layouts and documentation to identify requirements and solutions
	Write well-designed, testable code
	Produce specifications and determine operational feasibility
	Integrate software components into a fully functional software system
	Develop software verification plans and quality assurance procedures
	Document and maintain software functionality
	Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade existing systems
	Deploy programs and evaluate user feedback
	Comply with project plans and industry standards
	Ensure software is updated with latest features


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as a Software Engineer or Software Developer
	Experience designing interactive applications
	Ability to develop software in Java, Ruby on Rails, C++ or other programming languages
	Excellent knowledge of relational databases, SQL and ORM technologies (JPA2, Hibernate)
	Experience developing web applications using at least one popular web framework (JSF, Wicket, GWT, Spring MVC)
	Experience with test-driven development
	Proficiency in software engineering tools
	Ability to document requirements and specifications.


Salary
N100,000 - N160,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: globalaluminiumindustries@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Software Engineer Job at Global Aluminium Industries




        
    

        
        Global Aluminium Industries is a manufacturer of aluminum building components. The company is engaged in manufacturing and marketing building products including aluminium doors, windows, railings and suspended ceilings.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Software Engineer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking for a passionate Software Engineer to design, develop and install software solutions.
	Software Engineer responsibilities include gathering user requirements, defining system functionality and writing code in various languages, like Java, Ruby on Rails or .NET programming languages (e.g. C++ or JScript.NET.) Our ideal candidates are familiar with the software development life cycle (SDLC) from preliminary system analysis to tests and deployment.


Responsibilities


	Execute full software development life cycle (SDLC)
	Develop flowcharts, layouts and documentation to identify requirements and solutions
	Write well-designed, testable code
	Produce specifications and determine operational feasibility
	Integrate software components into a fully functional software system
	Develop software verification plans and quality assurance procedures
	Document and maintain software functionality
	Troubleshoot, debug and upgrade existing systems
	Deploy programs and evaluate user feedback
	Comply with project plans and industry standards
	Ensure software is updated with latest features


Requirements and skills


	Proven work experience as a Software Engineer or Software Developer
	Experience designing interactive applications
	Ability to develop software in Java, Ruby on Rails, C++ or other programming languages
	Excellent knowledge of relational databases, SQL and ORM technologies (JPA2, Hibernate)
	Experience developing web applications using at least one popular web framework (JSF, Wicket, GWT, Spring MVC)
	Experience with test-driven development
	Proficiency in software engineering tools
	Ability to document requirements and specifications.


Salary
N100,000 - N160,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HSE Officer",
  "description": "Fort Hermon Limited is a dynamic engineering and construction company specializing in delivering high-quality infrastructure, building, and industrial projects. With a focus on innovation, safety, and technical excellence, we provide end-to-end solutions that meet global standards and exceed client expectations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Location: Lagos&nbsp;
Employment Type: Contract

Description


	We are seeking a qualified and proactive HSE Officer to oversee health, safety, and environmental compliance on construction sites.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for implementing HSE policies, conducting safety inspections, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards, and promoting a strong safety culture across all project activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement and monitor Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) policies and procedures on site.
	Conduct daily site safety inspections and identify unsafe acts and conditions.
	Ensure all workers comply with company safety rules, use of PPE, and safe work practices.
	Carry out toolbox talks, safety inductions, and HSE training for employees and contractors.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly HSE reports.
	Investigate accidents, incidents, and near misses and recommend corrective actions.
	Monitor permit-to-work systems and ensure compliance for high-risk activities.
	Ensure proper emergency response procedures and first aid arrangements are in place.
	Conduct risk assessments and Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) for site activities.
	Ensure compliance with local safety regulations and company HSE standards.
	Monitor housekeeping and environmental management practices on site.
	Liaise with management, contractors, and regulatory authorities on HSE matters.
	Maintain HSE documentation, records, and statutory compliance requirements.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: vineonyido@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mall.",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-02",
  "employmentType": "Contract",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Fort Hermon Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",

HSE Officer Job at Fort Hermon Limited




        
    

        
        Fort Hermon Limited is a dynamic engineering and construction company specializing in delivering high-quality infrastructure, building, and industrial projects. With a focus on innovation, safety, and technical excellence, we provide end-to-end solutions that meet global standards and exceed client expectations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HSE Officer

Job Location: Lagos 
Employment Type: Contract

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a qualified and proactive HSE Officer to oversee health, safety, and environmental compliance on construction sites.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for implementing HSE policies, conducting safety inspections, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards, and promoting a strong safety culture across all project activities.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement and monitor Health, Safety, and Environmental (HSE) policies and procedures on site.
	Conduct daily site safety inspections and identify unsafe acts and conditions.
	Ensure all workers comply with company safety rules, use of PPE, and safe work practices.
	Carry out toolbox talks, safety inductions, and HSE training for employees and contractors.
	Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly HSE reports.
	Investigate accidents, incidents, and near misses and recommend corrective actions.
	Monitor permit-to-work systems and ensure compliance for high-risk activities.
	Ensure proper emergency response procedures and first aid arrangements are in place.
	Conduct risk assessments and Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) for site activities.
	Ensure compliance with local safety regulations and company HSE standards.
	Monitor housekeeping and environmental management practices on site.
	Liaise with management, contractors, and regulatory authorities on HSE matters.
	Maintain HSE documentation, records, and statutory compliance requirements.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mall.
